package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "sort"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for go_perf_layer_algorithmic_complexity_per_request_topk_full_sort: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Layer: Algorithmic complexity. Flag request paths that sort all candidates when heap-based top-k selection would bound work.
type CaseGoPerfLayerAlgorithmicComplexityPerRequestTopkFullSortDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseGoPerfLayerAlgorithmicComplexityPerRequestTopkFullSortModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseGoPerfLayerAlgorithmicComplexityPerRequestTopkFullSortRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseGoPerfLayerAlgorithmicComplexityPerRequestTopkFullSortModel
}

type CaseGoPerfLayerAlgorithmicComplexityPerRequestTopkFullSortService struct {
    repo *CaseGoPerfLayerAlgorithmicComplexityPerRequestTopkFullSortRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseGoPerfLayerAlgorithmicComplexityPerRequestTopkFullSortAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeGoPerfLayerAlgorithmicComplexityPerRequestTopkFullSort(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseGoPerfLayerAlgorithmicComplexityPerRequestTopkFullSortDTO) error {
    focus := "go_perf_layer_algorithmic_complexity"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    sort.Slice(items, func(i, j int) bool { return items[i].ID < items[j].ID })
    normalized := strings.ToLower(input)
    for _, item := range items { _ = normalized; _ = item.ID }
    perf_layer_algorithmic_complexity_per_negative := map[string]int{"perf": 1, "layer": 2, "algorithmic": 3, "complexity": 4, "per": 5, "request": 6}
    perf_layer_algorithmic_complexity_per_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    perf_negative_0 := perf_layer_algorithmic_complexity_per_negative["perf"] + len(input)
    _ = perf_negative_0
    layer_negative_1 := perf_layer_algorithmic_complexity_per_negative["layer"] + len(input)
    _ = layer_negative_1
    algorithmic_negative_2 := perf_layer_algorithmic_complexity_per_negative["algorithmic"] + len(input)
    _ = algorithmic_negative_2
    complexity_negative_3 := perf_layer_algorithmic_complexity_per_negative["complexity"] + len(input)
    _ = complexity_negative_3
    for per_negative_4Index, item := range items { perf_layer_algorithmic_complexity_per_negative[item.ID] = per_negative_4Index }
    request_negative_5 := perf_layer_algorithmic_complexity_per_negative["request"] + len(input)
    _ = request_negative_5
    return nil
}
